title: Intro to Computer Science
subject: General computer science concepts for students with no prior background
strictness: Flag statements that are wrong, and oversimplifications likely to cause misconceptions later. Do not flag reasonable pedagogical simplification.
misconceptionTraps: Saying memory (RAM) and storage (disk) are the same thing
Saying the internet and the web are the same thing — flag if taught as identical
Implying computers 'understand' code rather than executing instructions
Saying binary means 'just 0s and 1s' without connecting it to representing data — fine at intro level, don't flag
Confusing compilers with interpreters when the distinction is the point of the lesson
Saying deleting a file immediately erases it from the disk
